site.title: NRL TipBot Briefing 2026
site.subhead: Round 8 setup board for tipping, charity betting, and weekly NRL intel
site.intro: A weekly NRL command board for sharper tipping, cleaner bet tracking, and banking any wins for charity.

meta.description: Codex-managed NRL TipBot dashboard for Round 8 preview notes, charity betting discipline, and weekly NRL intel.

ticker: official round 8 team lists are now out and the board resets after the tigers charity miss
wests tigers head to head lost 21-20 to brisbane so the mh foundation bank slides back to $6.50
bulldogs over broncos looks like the cleanest straight tip with payne haas still sidelined and stephen crichton back
cowboys get reed mahoney, murray taulagi and jeremiah nanai back against a sharks side boosted by cam mcinnes
manly lose turbo to a hamstring injury before hosting the eels at 4 pines park
storm have dropped five straight and still need to prove they can be trusted
no round 8 charity play is locked yet

featurePanels.title: Lock Pick
items: Bulldogs head to head leads the early shortlist
Stephen Crichton is back while Payne Haas and Corey Jensen remain out for Brisbane
It looks like the cleanest straight tip on the board
The price may be too short for charity value, so do not force it
Wait for late mail before locking any Round 8 bet

featurePanels.title: Risk Flags
items: The Tigers charity miss was a reminder that close-game variance still bites
Turbo's hamstring injury changes the shape of Manly versus Parramatta
Cowboys versus Sharks is a hard game to price with major ins on both sides
Storm still carry danger even during a five-game slide
Do not chase last week's loss with a forced Round 8 play

featurePanels.title: Weekly Focus
items: Decide whether Bulldogs are worth trusting even if the market strips the value away
See if the Cowboys bounce back now that Mahoney, Taulagi and Nanai are back
Track whether Panthers absorb the Mitch Kenny loss cleanly
Watch if the Rabbitohs can keep the pressure on a wobbling Storm side
Keep the charity process selective after the first miss

featurePanels.title: Watchlist
items: Bulldogs still look like one of the calmest sides in the comp
Rabbitohs keep finding ways to stay relevant in tight games
Sharks may have a fresh ceiling with Cam McInnes back
Sea Eagles suddenly need to answer life without Turbo

betCard.title: Round 8 Charity Bet
betCard.pick: Bulldogs head to head leads the shortlist
why: Brisbane are still without Payne Haas and Corey Jensen through the middle.
Stephen Crichton returns and Canterbury still look more stable week to week.
The matchup reads clearly, but the likely price may be too short to offer proper charity value.
risks: Adam Reynolds can still drag Brisbane through ugly games.
The Bulldogs lose some punch with Jacob Kiraz sidelined.
If the market gets too short, the edge becomes too thin to bother with.

charity.title: Mark Hughes Foundation Ledger
charity.summary: All profits are earmarked for the Mark Hughes Foundation.
notes: Round 5: Newcastle head to head at $2.30 won and collected $11.50.
Round 6: North Queensland Cowboys head to head at $2.00 won and collected $10.00.
Round 7: Wests Tigers head to head at $1.53 lost in a 21-20 defeat to Brisbane.

watchlist: Bulldogs look increasingly trustworthy even when the market notices.
Sharks versus Cowboys may be the most deceptive game on the round.
Storm still need to earn trust back before they can be treated as automatic.
Manly without Turbo is one of the biggest variables on the board.

sections.title: Situation Report
sections.heading: Round 8 opens with a reset after the first charity miss
lines: As of Wednesday, 22 April 2026, official Round 8 team lists are now out and the board can reset without pretending last week did not sting.
The Round 7 Tigers charity bet lost 21-20 to Brisbane, so the Mark Hughes Foundation bank now sits at $6.50 with a 2-1 record.
That means the right move this week is discipline, not trying to win the loss back in one swing.

sections.title: Bet Analysis
sections.heading: Bulldogs lead the shortlist, but price discipline matters
lines: Canterbury still shapes as the cleanest straight winner with Crichton back and Brisbane missing Haas and Jensen through the middle.
The problem is price: if the market crushes Bulldogs too far, the edge may not be worth using for charity.
Cowboys versus Sharks and Tigers versus Raiders both have more swing and better upside, but also more ways to go wrong.
No Round 8 charity play should be locked until late mail and market shape are both checked.

sections.title: Form Radar
sections.heading: Who deserves the Round 8 upgrade or downgrade
lines: Cold: Storm, Dragons, Eels, the Broncos middle rotation.
Manly stay dangerous, but Turbo's injury means they cannot be treated the same way as last week.

sections.title: Intel Notes
sections.heading: The data points most worth checking before locking Round 8
lines: Payne Haas, Corey Jensen and Turbo are the three absence signals that most change how the board should be read.
The key returnees are Stephen Crichton for Canterbury, Cam McInnes for Cronulla, and the Cowboys trio of Nanai, Mahoney and Taulagi.
Penrith still lose Mitch Kenny, while the Warriors lose Charnze Nicoll-Klokstad and the Storm stay with the same squad despite five straight losses.
After the Tigers miss, do not lock any charity play until late mail and final prices are both in front of you.

footerNotes: Built from the attached Claude prototype and reorganised into a maintainable Codex workflow.
Use this as a weekly operating board, then swap in live-verified NRL data when you want to publish or bet.
